items.title: First conversation on claude.ai
items.scenario: A new user opens claude.ai, creates an account, and asks: "Explain what an API is to a product manager." Claude returns a structured explanation with an analogy and a short example—no code deployment required on the user's side.
items.explanation: This is the assistant experience: Claude runs remotely; the user only supplies a prompt and reads the reply.

items.title: Developer sends a Messages API request
items.scenario: An engineer uses the Anthropic Python SDK to POST a user message to the Messages API. The response contains assistant text blocks their backend forwards to a support ticket UI.
items.explanation: The same Claude models power both chat apps and API integrations—the interface changes, the core capability (language generation) does not.

items.title: Choosing a model tier for a task
items.scenario: A team routes quick FAQ replies through a faster, lower-cost Haiku-class model and sends complex contract analysis to a higher-capability Opus-class model.
items.explanation: Claude is a family: model choice is an engineering decision about latency, cost, and capability—not a different product brand.
